title: Daily Wrap — 22 May 2026
body: There were 60 races across 7 meetings, with action at Albion Park, Dubbo, Geelong, Gloucester Park, Mildura, Newcastle, and Wagga.

On the clock, **Rocktagonal** set the mark with a 1:52.0 mile rate over 1609 metres at Newcastle. For pure dominance, **Aldebaran Emjay**'s 18-metre win at Geelong was the one. It was not the only big-margin result either, with **Lou Will NZ** (18m at Newcastle) and **Royal Saxon** (15m at Mildura) also winning decisively.

The training story had a few threads. At Albion Park, **C A Turpin** prepared 4 winners; at Dubbo, **J T O'shea** completed a training double; at Gloucester Park, **G F J Bond, S A Bond** struck twice; at Mildura, **M L Gadsden** struck twice; at Newcastle, **G A Panella** finished with two winners; at Wagga, **E J Stewart** struck twice.

In the sulky, a few names kept coming up. At Albion Park, **Pete Mcmullen** finished with 4 winners; at Dubbo, **Jett Turnbull** finished with 3 winners and **John O'shea** and **Michael Jnr Day** both finished with two winners; at Newcastle, **Grace Panella** drove 3 winners; at Geelong, **James Herbertson** struck twice; at Gloucester Park, **Aiden De Campo**, **Deni Roberts**, and **Gary Hall Jnr** all finished with two winners; at Mildura, **Jack Laugher** and **Luke Dunne** both struck twice; at Wagga, **Cameron Hart** added a driving double.

There was also a milestone in the sulky, with **Jack Barton** with **Smack Bang Bart** at Wagga recording a first driving win. On the training side, **C L Barton** with **Smack Bang Bart** at Wagga landed a first training win.

The roughest winner was **Smack Bang Bart**, landing at $23 at Wagga. It was not all plain sailing for the short elects. **Fire and Passion** ($1.15) went under at Albion Park, **Cardigan Jem** ($1.28) went under at Wagga, **Butter Cup** ($1.35) went under at Dubbo, and Newcastle saw **Next To Me NZ** ($1.35) and **Rocknroll Rocket** ($1.50) beaten.
Leaders had mixed fortunes across the meetings. Front-runners had the better of it at Albion Park (7 of 11) and Gloucester Park (6 of 10).

One of the standout performances in defeat came from **Cardigan Dan**, who produced the day’s fastest calculated finishing burst — an estimated individual last half of 53.8 — despite finishing 2nd at Wagga.

body: The day’s harness action stretched across 6 meetings — Albion Park, Gloucester Park, Menangle, Shepparton, Terang, and Wagga — producing 50 races in total.

On the clock, LULU MAE set the mark with a 1:51.1 mile rate over 1660 metres at Albion Park. For pure dominance, SUPERIOR STRIDE's 31-metre win at Menangle was the one. It was not the only big-margin result either, with CENTURION DREAM (11m at Terang) also winning decisively.

There were several training doubles to note. At Menangle, R J Alchin prepared 3 winners and K A Morris struck twice; at Albion Park, C A Turpin struck twice; at Gloucester Park, G F J Bond, S A Bond struck twice; at Terang, M A Lee, P D Lee prepared a winning pair.

In the sulky, a few names kept coming up. At Albion Park, Nathan Dawson and Pete McMullen both finished with 3 winners; at Gloucester Park, Deni Roberts drove a double; at Menangle, Declan Murphy finished with two winners; at Shepparton, Nathan Jack drove a double; at Terang, Jason Lee drove a double.

The biggest market surprise came via PLAYA BLANCA, who scored at $21 at Menangle. It was not all plain sailing for the short elects. Gloucester Park saw KATAMA NZ ($1.28) and TRUE GRIT NZ ($1.60) beaten, HILLVIEW SAM ($1.60) went under at Wagga, and Terang saw CEE WHOS BETTOR NZ ($1.65) and HUXHAM CHUBB ($1.95) beaten.
The race-shape picture was not uniform. Leaders found it harder at Gloucester Park (2 of 8) and Wagga (1 of 9).

One of the standout performances in defeat came from OUR SUNSET DELIGHT, who produced the day’s fastest calculated finishing burst — an estimated individual last half of 54.3 — despite finishing 2nd at Albion Park.

title: Daily Wrap — 4 May 2026
body: There were 29 races across 4 meetings, with action at Canberra, Globe Derby Park, Marburg, and Pinjarra.

On the clock, APPLAUDING set the mark with a 1:56.2 mile rate over 1770 metres at Canberra. For pure dominance, DIDMESELFAMISCHIEF's 26-metre win at Globe Derby Park was the one. It was not the only big-margin result either, with AMERICAN EYES (20m at Pinjarra) and APPLAUDING (15m at Canberra) also winning decisively.
The training story had a few threads. At Canberra, N A Turnbull struck twice; at Marburg, A J Finnis finished with two winners; at Pinjarra, D Egerton-Green struck twice.

The driving honours were spread across the card. At Canberra, Jett Turnbull drove a double; at Globe Derby Park, Wayne Hill drove a double; at Marburg, Gary Bond drove a double; at Pinjarra, Dylan Egerton-Green and Gary Hall Jnr both drove doubles.

The biggest market surprise came via SONNY RENNT, who scored at $71 at Globe Derby Park. Short-priced runners also found trouble. Marburg saw SPLASHNDASH ($1.35), LANEYS LAST ($1.40), MUCH BETTOR NZ ($1.70), and TOODALOU ($1.90) beaten and SPIRIT OF MARMYTE NZ ($1.45) went under at Globe Derby Park.
Leaders had mixed fortunes across the meetings. Those in front were hard to catch at Pinjarra (5 of 8); while leaders found it harder at Globe Derby Park (1 of 8).

One of the standout performances in defeat came from VINITA ROSE NZ, who produced the day’s fastest calculated finishing burst — an estimated individual last half of 55.8 — despite finishing 2nd at Pinjarra.

title: Daily Wrap — 3 May 2026
body: There were 32 races across 4 meetings, with action at Central Wheatbelt, Forbes, Hobart, and Shepparton.

On the clock, MAGNETIC TERROR NZ set the mark with a 1:54.9 mile rate over 1609 metres at Hobart. For pure dominance, BARBER SHOP's 20-metre win at Hobart was the one. It was not the only big-margin result either, with TAKEN BY WINE (15m at Shepparton) and ALL OVER IT (13m at Shepparton) also winning decisively.

The trainers' column had more than one talking point. At Central Wheatbelt, G F J Bond, S A Bond finished with two winners; at Forbes, D L Davis prepared a winning pair; at Hobart, H P Woods and W J Yole both prepared winning pairs.

The driving honours were spread across the card. At Central Wheatbelt, Deni Roberts drove a double; at Forbes, Blake Medlyn and Jett Turnbull both struck twice; at Hobart, Grace Jones and Heath Woods both finished with two winners; at Shepparton, Ryan Sanderson struck twice.

The roughest winner was COCOSWORLD, landing at $34 at Shepparton. Favourite backers had a few bruises as well. IMON NZ ($1.25) went under at Shepparton, STUDLEIGH STARLITE ($1.55) went under at Forbes, ALOT LIKE PENDLES ($1.70) went under at Hobart, and SANFORD HARRY ($1.95) went under at Central Wheatbelt.
The maps played out differently depending on venue. Front-runners had the better of it at Hobart (6 of 9) and Shepparton (5 of 8).

One of the standout performances in defeat came from DUN SPRUIKIN, who produced the day’s fastest calculated finishing burst — an estimated individual last half of 56.6 — despite finishing 8th at Forbes.

title: Daily Wrap — 2 May 2026
body: 5 meetings — Albion Park, Bunbury, Globe Derby Park, Melton, and Menangle — made up the day’s 45-race harness action.

On the clock, SOHO GUNSLINGER set the mark with a 1:48.9 mile rate over 1609 metres at Menangle. For pure dominance, IDEAL AN ACE's 19-metre win at Bunbury was the one. It was not the only big-margin result either, with SEA SALT (16m at Globe Derby Park) and ROCKINWITHATTITUDE (13m at Menangle) also winning decisively.
The training story had a few threads. At Globe Derby Park, J K Neilson, K Wilson finished with two winners; at Melton, A A Herbertson finished with two winners.

The driving honours were spread across the card. At Melton, James Herbertson drove 3 winners; at Albion Park, Angus Garrard, Layne Dwyer, and Tom Callaghan all added driving doubles; at Bunbury, Emily Suvaljko added a driving double; at Globe Derby Park, Kevin Weidenbach and Ryan Hryhorec both drove doubles; at Menangle, Ashleigh Delosa and Cameron Hart both added driving doubles.

The roughest winner was MANILA PLAYBOY, landing at $31 at Albion Park. It was not all plain sailing for the short elects. JILLIBY BALLERINI ($1.20) went under at Menangle, Bunbury saw STARRY HEIGHTS ($1.50) and RUIA NZ ($1.50) beaten, and Albion Park saw OAKLEY SHARD NZ ($1.65) and DEBBIE LINCOLN NZ ($1.70) beaten.
From a race-shape point of view, the day had a bit of contrast. Leaders were rewarded at Menangle (7 of 11); while leaders found it harder at Melton (0 of 9).

One of the standout performances in defeat came from CAN BE A RUEBE NZ, who produced the day’s fastest calculated finishing burst — an estimated individual last half of 53.2 — despite finishing 5th at Menangle.

title: Daily Wrap — 1 May 2026
body: 7 meetings — Albury, Gloucester Park, Launceston, Melton, Mildura, Newcastle, and Redcliffe — made up the day’s 57-race harness action.

On the clock, SPUNKYS GOTSECRETS set the mark with a 1:53.1 mile rate over 1609 metres at Newcastle. For pure dominance, HERES HERBIE NZ's 23-metre win at Gloucester Park was the one. It was not the only big-margin result either, with EYE SEE AMOUR (21m at Launceston) and BLACK GEMSTONE (17m at Redcliffe) also winning decisively.

There were several training doubles to note. At Gloucester Park, G F J Bond, S A Bond prepared 3 winners; at Launceston, T R Langley prepared 3 winners; at Melton, A P Gath finished with two winners; at Mildura, T H McGuigan struck twice; at Newcastle, K A Morris finished with two winners.

The drivers had their share of repeat winners too. At Gloucester Park, Deni Roberts drove 3 winners; at Launceston, Dylan Ford drove 3 winners; at Mildura, Jackie Barker drove 3 winners and Jack Laugher and Wayne Hill both struck twice; at Albury, Tasmyn Potter drove a double; at Melton, James Herbertson and Matthew Whittle both drove doubles; at Newcastle, Brad Elder, Robert Morris, and Seaton Grima all added driving doubles; at Redcliffe, Jonah Hutchinson and Nathan Dawson both finished with two winners.

LACEYS LAD NZ turned the market on its head at Newcastle, getting home at $31. It was not all plain sailing for the short elects. WHERE IT BEGAN ($1.22) went under at Albury, KING OF NORMANDY ($1.35) went under at Melton, Newcastle saw SUGAR PIE HONEY ($1.40) and RAYS REDEMPTION ($1.40) beaten, and ZOE ELLA ($1.50) went under at Launceston.
The maps played out differently depending on venue. Leaders were rewarded at Mildura (5 of 8) and Redcliffe (5 of 8); while front-runners had less control at Albury (1 of 8) and Launceston (1 of 7).

One of the standout performances in defeat came from PETES CRUNCH TIME, who produced the day’s fastest calculated finishing burst — an estimated individual last half of 55.5 — despite finishing 3rd at Newcastle.
